title: URL Allowlisting, Untrusted API Data, Terminal Safety, Files, and Resource Abuse · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Revalidate scheme, host, port, redirect count, and destination policy on every hop and reject user-controlled egress.
Normalize or visibly escape control, bidi, and ANSI sequences from API, file, and user data before terminal presentation.
Choose application-owned storage roots, reject traversal or unexpected file types, and minimize permission and symlink assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Redirect Egres…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 7298 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce destination, terminal, file, privacy, and resource trust boundaries; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 2250 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce destination, terminal, file, privacy, and resource trust boundaries; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound command length, response bytes, decoded records, cache, history, collection, concurrency, retries, output, and persistence size.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pokedex Go Resource Abuse….

options.text: Keep the plausible Pokedex Go Resource Abuse Defense output and hide the violated deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Pokedex Go Resource Abuse Defense.
